source_metadata.title_raw: Shawn Hagerty - A-19-200 - October 3, 2019 - San Diego

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

October 3, 2019

Shawn Hagerty
Best Best & Krieger LLP
City Attorney
City of Santee
655 West Broadway, 15th Floor
San Diego, CA 92101

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-19-200

Dear Mr. Hagerty:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of interest or Section 1090. Also note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is not the case or if the facts should change, you should contact us for additional advice.

The Commission does not provide advice regarding past conduct. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A).) Accordingly, nothing in this letter should be construed to evaluate any past conduct, and any conclusions contained in this letter apply only to prospective actions.

QUESTION

Do the Act's conflict of interest provisions prohibit Council Member Laura Koval from taking part in decisions relating to a proposed mixed-use project where her employer, Padre Dam Municipal Water District ("Water District") owns property adjacent to the project?

CONCLUSION

No. As explained below, Council Member Koval does not have an interest in her government employer, and therefore the Act's conflict of interest provisions do not prohibit her from taking part in decisions relating to the project.

FACTS AS PRESENTED BY REQUESTER

___________________________

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-19-200
Page No. 2

Your firm serves as City Attorney for the City of Santee ("City") and you seek this advice
on behalf of City Council Member Koval who is employed by the Water District as the Director of
Parks and Recreation for Santee Lakes Recreation Preserve ("Santee Lakes"), the Water District's
recreational and camping area. Council Member Koval receives a salary from the Water District.

The boundaries of the City overlap with those of the Water District, and Santee Lakes is
located entirely within the City. A developer has submitted to the City an application for a mixed-
use project on the 2,600-acre site known as Fanita Ranch (the "Project"), which abuts the Santee
Lakes property (the two properties share common property lines on the west and south side of the
Project.) The Project would include the development of 2,949 dwelling units and 80,000 square feet
of commercial and retail space. The dwelling units closest to Santee Lakes would be located
approximately 420 feet from the Santee Lakes property line.

The City has retained a consultant to prepare the Environmental Impact Report ("EIR")
for the Project. As part of the process of preparing the EIR, the consultant has discussed with the
Water District the potential impacts of the Project on Santee Lakes. Due to the close proximity, it is
likely that the Project will result in certain impacts to Santee Lakes, including impacts related to
noise and traffic.

In her role as Director of Park and Recreation, Council Member Koval has provided
information regarding the impact of the Project on Santee Lakes to Water District employees who
are participating in the discussions with the consultant. Council Member Koval has not personally
corresponded or met with the consultant and does not foresee such contact in the future. As the
Project proceeds, the developer's various applications will come before the City Council for
approval including, applications for a General Plan Amendment, Specific Plan, zone district
reclassification and Development Review Permit.

ANALYSIS

Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or
otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the
official has a financial interest. Section 87103 provides that an official has a "financial interest" in a
decision, within the meaning of the Act, if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a
material financial effect on one or more of the official's interests identified in that section.

Section 87103 identifies interests from which a conflict of interest may arise. Most pertinent
to the analysis are the following:

• An interest in any source of income to the official aggregating $500 or more in
value provided to, received by, or promised to, the public official within 12 months prior to
the decision.

• An interest in a business entity in which the public official is a director, officer,
partner, trustee, employee, or holds any position of management.

File No. A-19-200
Page No. 3

You state that Council Member Koval is employed by the Water District as the Director of
Parks and Recreation for Santee Lakes. Presumably, she will receive more than $500 from the
Water District in the 12 months prior to any decision related to the Project coming before the City
Council.

However, under Section 82030(b)(2) salary, reimbursement for expenses, and per diem from
a governmental agency are not considered income for purposes of the Act. Additionally,
governmental agencies, such as the Water District, are not organizations or enterprises operated for
profit. Therefore, Council Member Koval does not have an interest in the Water District, either as a
source of income or a business entity. Barring any other interest in the decision, Council Member
Koval is not prohibited from taking part in the decisions related to the project.²

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Dave Bainbridge
General Counsel

By: Jack Woodside
Counsel, Legal Division

JW:aja

² While we note that an official also has a financial interest in his or her personal finances (Section 87103;
Regulation 18702.5), there is also no indication that the identified decisions will foreseeably affect the Council
Member's personal finances.

sections.question:
Do the Act's conflict of interest provisions prohibit Council Member Laura Koval from 
taking part in decisions relating to a proposed mixed-use project where her employer, Padre Dam 
Municipal Water District ("Water District") owns property adjacent to the project?
sections.conclusion:
No. As explained below, Council Member Koval does not have an interest in her 
government employer, and therefore the Act's conflict of interest provisions do not prohibit her 
from taking part in decisions relating to the project.

sections.analysis:
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or 
otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the 
official has a financial interest. Section 87103 provides that an official has a "financial interest" in a 
decision, within the meaning of the Act, if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a

• An interest in any source of income to the official aggregating $500 or more in 
value provided to, received by, or promised to, the public official within 12 months prior to

partner, trustee, employee, or holds any position of management.  
You state that Council Member Koval is employed by the Water District as the Director of 
Parks and Recreation for Santee Lakes. Presumably, she will receive more than $500 from the 
Water District in the 12 months prior to any decision related to the Project coming before the City 
Council. 
However, under Section 82030(b )(2) salary, reimbursement for expenses, and per diem from 
a governmental agency are not considered income for purposes of the Act. Additionally, 
government agencies, such as the Water District, are not organizations or enterprises operated for 
profit. Therefore, Council Member Koval does not have an interest in the Water District, either as a 
source of income or a business entity. Barring any other interest in the decision, Council Member 
Koval is not prohibited from taking part in the decisions related to the project.2 
JW:aja

taking part in decisions relating to a proposed mixed-use project where her employer, Padre Dam

CONCLUSION: No. As explained below, Council Member Koval does not have an interest in her 
government employer, and therefore the Act's conflict of interest provisions do not prohibit her 
from taking part in decisions relating to the project.
